# PMD Core Ontology (PMDco) Documentation

> PMDco is a mid-level ontology for materials science and engineering (MSE), developed by the Platform MaterialDigital (PMD). It provides reusable, BFO-aligned classes, properties, usage patterns and SHACL shapes for representing materials, processes, measurements and simulations in knowledge graphs.

This file helps AI agents and LLMs navigate the documentation. Each link points to a published documentation page; see llms-full.txt for the complete text of every page in a single file.

## Getting Started

- [Home](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/index.html): Welcome to the Platform MaterialDigital Core Ontology (PMDco), your gateway to advancing the digital transformation of Materials Science and Engineering (MSE)! As the field evolves, managing complex…
- [Introduction](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/intro.html): The Platform MaterialDigital Core Ontology (PMDco) is a mid-level ontology specifically developed to support the digital transformation of the Materials Science and Engineering (MSE) domain. With its…
- [Introduction to Ontologies](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/introduction-to-ontologies.html): An ontology is a formally defined logical structure that represents concepts, categories, properties, and relationships within a specific scientific domain using standardized definitions and naming…

## Ontology Guide

- [Ontology Structure](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/ontology-structure.html): Furthermore, in ODK-based ontology modularization, modules such as import-edit, shared, and axioms-shared play distinct roles. The import-edit module contains external ontology terms and their…
- [Usage Patterns](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/patterns.html): In ontology development and usage, usage patterns address recurring modeling requirements by providing standardized, reusable semantic snippets. These patterns ensure consistent representation of…
- [User Guide](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/pmdco-user-guide.html): Although several guidelines and tutorials for ontology development are available (see Ontology Learning Materials for Beginners), we provide here a dedicated list of recommendations for creating…
- [Term Obsoletion](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/obsolete-ontology-terms.html): Ontologies evolve, terms are revised, split into more detailed terms or deprecated. For proper reusablity of the ontology and traceability of the reason why it was deleted and what is a possible…
- [Reused Ontologies](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/reused-ontologies.html): The PMDco complements and extends several existing ontologies by providing a mid-level framework tailored for the MSE domain. The reused ontologies listed below:

## Development

- [Glossary & Tools](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/glossary-and-tools.html): The PMDco development is made with the help of the ontology development kit (ODK). The ODK is an incredibly great tool to manage your ontology's life cycle. The ODK is:
- [Migration](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/migration.html): Because PMDco 3.x is based on BFO, the migration is not a simple translation but a re-modeling of the class hierarchy. The safest approach is to relocate each major conceptual block of the old…

- [Versions](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/versions.html): The latest version of the ontology can always be found at:
- [Publications](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/publications.html): The study focusing on the tensile test ontology (TTO), which semantically represents the mechanical tensile test method and is developed within the project Plattform MaterialDigital (PMD): -…
- [References](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/references.html): Resource Description Framework

## About

- [Who We Are](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/who-we-are.html): Their work focuses on creating and sustaining frameworks that represent materials data in a consistent and meaningful way, enabling experimental, simulation, and characterization data to be linked,…
- [Acknowledgements](https://materialdigital.github.io/core-ontology/docs/acknowledgements.html): The authors thank the Federal Government and the Heads of Government of the Länder for their funding and support within the framework of the Plattform MaterialDigital. Funded by the German Federal…

## Ontology files & resources

- [PMDco full ontology (TTL)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/materialdigital/core-ontology/main/pmdco.ttl): complete ontology in Turtle.
- [PMDco full ontology (OWL)](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/materialdigital/core-ontology/main/pmdco.owl): complete ontology in RDF/XML.
- [Modeling patterns](https://github.com/materialdigital/core-ontology/tree/main/patterns): reusable usage patterns with example data (TTL) and SHACL shapes.
- [Repository](https://github.com/materialdigital/core-ontology): source, issues and contribution guide.
